title: ZooKeeper Connection Count by Client IP
dataSources: `zk_num_alive_connections` from `zookeeper:mntr` plus `zookeeper:log` client connect lines if enriched
app: If client IPs are unavailable centrally, enrich via `cons` four-letter output scripted to `zookeeper:conf` custom sourcetype; otherwise track aggregate connections
spl:
index=infrastructure sourcetype="zookeeper:conf"
| rex field=_raw "/(?<client_ip>\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+):(?<client_port>\d+)"
| stats dc(client_port) as sessions by client_ip, host
| where sessions > 500
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: A single client IP opening hundreds of sessions usually signals a connection leak or misconfigured pool, which can exhaust file descriptors and starve legitimate brokers.
value: Protects shared ensembles from accidental denial-of-service by one application release.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to protect shared ensembles from accidental denial-of-service by one application release.
implementation: Whitelist `cons` in `zoo.cfg`; schedule read-only `cons` dumps to Splunk with sourcetype `zookeeper:conf` (rename if you prefer `zookeeper:cons`). Sanitize payloads; restrict index ACLs. Alternative: alert only on `zk_num_alive_connections` spikes from `mntr` when `cons` is disallowed.
visualization: Table (client_ip, sessions), Bar chart top talkers.
knownFalsePositives: Spikes or gaps can follow approved maintenance, load tests, or short collection outages. We add a second signal and a change window before escalating.
